sources.title: Pearson Edexcel Level 3 Advanced Subsidiary GCE in Mathematics specification

statements.statementText: The aims and objectives of this qualification are to enable students to: ● understand mathematics and mathematical processes in a way that promotes confidence, fosters enjoyment and provides a strong foundation for progress to further study
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r0-b2-l0,p10-r0-b3-l0,p10-r1-b0-l0, p10-aims

statements.statementText: The aims and objectives of this qualification are to enable students to: ● recognise when mathematics can be used to analyse and solve a problem in context
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r0-b2-l0,p10-r10-b0-l0, p10-aims

statements.statementText: The aims and objectives of this qualification are to enable students to: ● represent situations mathematically and understand the relationship between problems in context and mathematical models that may be applied to solve them
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r0-b2-l0,p10-r10-b1-l0,p10-r11-b0-l0, p10-aims

statements.statementText: The aims and objectives of this qualification are to enable students to: ● draw diagrams and sketch graphs to help explore mathematical situations and interpret solutions
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r0-b2-l0,p10-r12-b0-l0,p10-r13-b0-l0, p10-aims

statements.statementText: The aims and objectives of this qualification are to enable students to: ● make deductions and inferences and draw conclusions by using mathematical reasoning
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r0-b2-l0,p10-r14-b0-l0, p10-aims

statements.statementText: The aims and objectives of this qualification are to enable students to: ● interpret solutions and communicate their interpretation effectively in the context of the problem
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r0-b2-l0,p10-r14-b1-l0,p10-r15-b0-l0, p10-aims

statements.statementText: The aims and objectives of this qualification are to enable students to: ● read and comprehend mathematical arguments, including justifications of methods and formulae, and communicate their understanding
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r0-b2-l0,p10-r16-b0-l0,p10-r17-b0-l0, p10-aims

statements.statementText: The aims and objectives of this qualification are to enable students to: ● read and comprehend articles concerning applications of mathematics and communicate their understanding
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r0-b2-l0,p10-r18-b0-l0,p10-r19-b0-l0, p10-aims

statements.statementText: The aims and objectives of this qualification are to enable students to: ● use technology, such as calculators and computers, effectively and recognise when it may be inappropriate to use them
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r0-b2-l0,p10-r20-b0-l0,p10-r21-b0-l0, p10-aims

statements.statementText: The aims and objectives of this qualification are to enable students to: ● take increasing responsibility for their own learning and the evaluation of their own mathematical development.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r0-b2-l0,p10-r22-b0-l0,p10-r23-b0-l0, p10-aims

statements.statementText: The aims and objectives of this qualification are to enable students to: ● extend their range of mathematical skills and techniques
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r0-b2-l0,p10-r2-b0-l0, p10-aims

statements.statementText: The aims and objectives of this qualification are to enable students to: ● understand coherence and progression in mathematics and how different areas of mathematics are connected
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r0-b2-l0,p10-r2-b1-l0,p10-r3-b0-l0, p10-aims

statements.statementText: The aims and objectives of this qualification are to enable students to: ● apply mathematics in other fields of study and be aware of the relevance of mathematics to the world of work and to situations in society in general
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r0-b2-l0,p10-r4-b0-l0,p10-r5-b0-l0, p10-aims

statements.statementText: The aims and objectives of this qualification are to enable students to: ● use their mathematical knowledge to make logical and reasoned decisions in solving problems both within pure mathematics and in a variety of contexts, and communicate the mathematical rationale for these decisions clearly
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r0-b2-l0,p10-r6-b0-l0,p10-r7-b0-l0,p10-r7-b0-l1, p10-aims

statements.statementText: The aims and objectives of this qualification are to enable students to: ● reason logically and recognise incorrect reasoning
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r0-b2-l0,p10-r8-b0-l0, p10-aims

statements.statementText: The aims and objectives of this qualification are to enable students to: ● generalise mathematically
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r0-b2-l0,p10-r8-b1-l0, p10-aims

statements.statementText: The aims and objectives of this qualification are to enable students to: ● construct mathematical proofs
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r0-b2-l0,p10-r8-b2-l0, p10-aims

statements.statementText: The aims and objectives of this qualification are to enable students to: ● use their mathematical skills and techniques to solve challenging problems that require them to decide on the solution strategy
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 10, line IDs p10-r0-b2-l0,p10-r8-b3-l0,p10-r9-b0-l0, p10-aims

statements.topicHeading: Overarching theme 1: Mathematical argument, language and proof
statements.statementText: AS Mathematics students must use the mathematical notation set out in the booklet Mathematical Formulae and Statistical Tables and be able to recall the mathematical formulae and identities set out in Appendix 1.

statements.statementText: Comprehend and critique mathematical arguments, proofs and justifications of methods and formulae, including those relating to applications of mathematics.

statements.statementText: Recognise the underlying mathematical structure in a situation and simplify and abstract appropriately to enable problems to be solved.

reviewNotes: Directly matches recognising mathematical structure.

reviewNotes: Exactly describes abstraction and simplification.

statements.statementText: Construct extended arguments to solve problems presented in an unstructured form, including problems in context.

reviewNotes: Direct reference to unstructured problems.

statements.statementText: Interpret and communicate solutions in the context of the original problem.

statements.statementText: Understand the concept of a mathematical problem-solving cycle, including specifying the problem, collecting information, processing and representing information and interpreting results, which may identify the need to repeat the cycle.

statements.statementText: Pearson has provided a large data set, which will support the assessment of statistics in Paper 2: Statistics and Mechanics.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 13, line IDs p13-r0-b1-l0,p13-r0-b1-l1, LDS

statements.statementText: The data set can be downloaded from our website, qualifications.pearson.com.

statements.statementText: This data set should be appropriate for the lifetime of the qualification.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 13, line IDs p13-r8-b1-l0,p13-r8-b1-l1, LDS

statements.statementText: However we will review the data set on an annual basis to ensure it is appropriate.

statements.statementText: If we need to make changes to the data set, we will notify centres before the beginning of the one-year course before students complete their examination.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 13, line IDs p13-r8-b1-l2,p13-r8-b1-l3,p13-r8-b1-l4, LDS

statements.statementText: Students are required to become familiar with the data set in advance of the final assessment.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 13, line IDs p13-r0-b1-l1,p13-r0-b1-l2, LDS

statements.statementText: Assessments will be designed in such a way that questions assume knowledge and understanding of the data set.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 13, line IDs p13-r0-b2-l0,p13-r0-b2-l1, LDS

statements.statementText: The expectation is that these questions should be likely to give a material advantage to students who have studied and are familiar with the data set.

statements.statementText: They might include questions/tasks that: ● assume familiarity with the terminology and contexts of the data, and do not explain them in a way that gives students who have not studied the data set the same opportunities to access marks as students who have studied them
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 13, line IDs p13-r0-b2-l3,p13-r0-b3-l0,p13-r1-b0-l0,p13-r1-b0-l1, LDS

statements.statementText: They might include questions/tasks that: ● use summary statistics or selected data from, or statistical diagrams based on, the data set – these might be given in the question/task, or as stimulus materials
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 13, line IDs p13-r0-b2-l3,p13-r2-b0-l0,p13-r3-b0-l0, LDS

statements.statementText: They might include questions/tasks that: ● are based on samples related to the contexts in the data set, where students’ work with the data set will help them understand the background context, and/or
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 13, line IDs p13-r0-b2-l3,p13-r4-b0-l0,p13-r5-b0-l0, LDS

statements.statementText: They might include questions/tasks that: ● require students to interpret data in ways that would be too demanding in an unfamiliar context.
statements.sourceLocator: Official specification PDF page 13, line IDs p13-r0-b2-l3,p13-r6-b0-l0,p13-r7-b0-l0, LDS

statements.statementText: Students will not be required to have copies of the data set in the examination, nor will they be required to have detailed knowledge of the actual data within the data set.

statements.statementText: Understand and use the structure of mathematical proof, proceeding from given assumptions through a series of logical steps to a conclusion; use methods of proof, including: Proof by deduction
examplesText: Examples of proofs: Proof by deduction, e.g. using completion of the square, prove that n 2 − 6n + 10 is positive for all values of n or, for example, differentiation from first principles for small positive integer powers of x . This is the most commonly used method of proof throughout this specification.

statements.statementText: Solve simultaneous equations in two variables by elimination and by substitution, including one linear and one quadratic equation.
examplesText: The quadratic may involve powers of 2 in one unknown or in both unknowns, e.g. solve y = 2x + 3, y = x² − 4x + 8 or 2x − 3y = 6, x² − y² + 3x = 50.

statements.statementText: Solve linear and quadratic inequalities in a single variable and interpret such inequalities graphically, including inequalities with brackets and fractions.
notesText: For example, solving ax + b > cx + d, px² + qx + r ≥ 0, px² + qx + r < ax + b and interpreting the third inequality as the range of x for which the curve y = px² + qx + r is below the line with equation y = ax + b. These would be reducible to linear or quadratic inequalities, e.g. a/x < b becomes ax < bx².

reviewNotes: Example of solving a linear inequality.
Example of solving and interpreting a quadratic inequality.

statements.statementText: Express solutions through correct use of ‘and’ and ‘or’, or through set notation.
notesText: So, e.g. x < a or x > b is equivalent to {x : x < a} ∪ {x : x > b} and {x : c < x} ∩ {x : x < d} is equivalent to x > c and x < d

statements.statementText: Understand and use Newton’s second law for motion in a straight line (restricted to forces in two perpendicular directions or simple cases of forces given as 2-D vectors).
notesText: Problems will involve motion in a straight line with constant acceleration in scalar form, where the forces act either parallel or perpendicular to the motion. Resolving forces is not required. Problems will involve motion in a straight line with constant acceleration in vector form, where the forces are given in i − j form or as column vectors.

reviewNotes: Directly matches Newton's second law.
Forces given as column vectors as per notes.

statements.statementText: Understand and use weight and motion in a straight line under gravity; gravitational acceleration, g, and its value in S.I. units to varying degrees of accuracy.
notesText: The default value of g will be 9.8 m s⁻² but some questions may specify another value, e.g. g = 10 m s⁻². The inverse square law for gravitation is not required and g may be assumed to be constant, but students should be aware that g is not a universal constant but depends on location.
